candidates.title: Rate-Distortion Theory + Cross-Domain Characterization + ASI + Phase Transition
candidates.summary: A unified research program treating absorption as rate-distortion optimal behavior under flat sparsity penalties. Core contributions: (1) formal proof of absorption optimality with closed-form threshold lambda > sin^2(theta_{p,c}), with the counterintuitive and falsifiable prediction that co-occurrence frequency cancels; (2) first cross-domain absorption characterization on 4 hierarchy types (spelling, entity-type, geographic, grammatical) with rigorous shuffled-label null controls, bootstrap CIs, and Bonferroni correction; (3) probe-free Absorption Susceptibility Index computed from decoder geometry + activation frequencies; (4) phase transition characterization with hysteresis test. All components are primarily training-free analysis on pre-trained Gemma Scope SAEs.
hypotheses: Feature absorption is rate-distortion optimal when lambda > sin^2(theta_{p,c}), where theta is the decoder angle between parent and child features; co-occurrence frequency cancels
Absorption occurs at statistically detectable rates in at least 3 of 4 tested hierarchy types (spelling, entity-type, geographic, grammatical) after Bonferroni correction
ASI = cos^2(theta_{p,c}) x (freq_p / freq_c) predicts absorbed feature pairs with AUROC >= 0.70 without probe training
Absorption onset is a phase transition in the sparsity parameter, potentially with hysteresis (metastable absorbed state)
candidates.pilot_focus: 15-minute pilot on GPT-2 Small: (1) load SAELens SAE, (2) compute ASI for all co-active pairs (filter to co-activation > 0.01 ~10k pairs), (3) cross-reference top-100 pairs against Neuronpedia labels, (4) measure first-letter absorption rate. Validates the computational pipeline and gives early signal on ASI predictive value.
primary_gaps_addressed: Gap 1 (no quantitative theory of absorption severity)
Gap 2 (absorption only measured on first-letter spelling task)
Gap 3 (no rigorous null controls in existing absorption measurements)
key_risks: ASI has low predictive power (AUROC < 0.60) -- mitigate by retaining as supplementary if it fails
Cross-domain absorption rates indistinguishable from null -- publishable as informative null result
Rate-distortion threshold AUROC < 0.65 -- reframe as existence proof + qualitative predictions still publishable
L0 misspecification confounds cross-domain results -- addressed by Phase C.4 deconfounding control

perspective_weights.theoretical: HIGH -- provided rigorous proof structure, biconvex decomposition argument for local-vs-global optimality gap, exact impossibility theorem formula with iterated projections
perspective_weights.innovator: HIGH -- rate-distortion framing, ASI concept, cross-domain hypothesis, lateral inhibition as theoretical corollary
perspective_weights.empiricist: HIGH -- shuffled-label controls, Bonferroni correction, bootstrap CIs, probe quality gate, random-SAE baseline all adopted
perspective_weights.pragmatist: MEDIUM-HIGH -- cross-domain code path (sae-spelling + RAVEL), L0 diagnostic control ('Sparse but Wrong'), feasibility validation
perspective_weights.contrarian: MEDIUM -- L0 deconfounding control incorporated as Phase C.4; seed-dependence concern noted but deprioritized for canonical Gemma Scope SAEs
perspective_weights.interdisciplinary: MEDIUM -- phase transition and hysteresis from Theoretical + Interdisciplinary; immunodominance analogy retained as interpretive framing in discussion

candidates.title: Absorption-as-Representational-Diagnostic
candidates.summary: Reframe absorption from a failure mode to a diagnostic signal. The absorption graph (absorber->absorbee relationships) encodes the model's internal feature hierarchy. Cross-layer absorption profiles reveal computational structure: early layers show less absorption (context-independent features); middle layers show more (contextually entangled). Mitigation methods reduce absorption rates by explicitly encoding hierarchy rather than by genuinely recovering previously absorbed features.
hypotheses: Absorption rate correlates positively with hierarchical entanglement across layers and domains
The absorption graph has non-trivial clustering structure reflecting semantic relationships and is consistent across SAE widths
Cross-layer absorption profile has systematic structure (peaks at middle layers) consistent with known properties of layer-wise representations
Matryoshka's absorption reduction is partially due to explicit hierarchy encoding rather than genuine recovery
candidates.pilot_focus: 30-minute pilot: measure absorption rate on first-letter task across layers 3, 6, 12, 19 for Gemma 2 2B with 16k and 65k SAEs. Test whether cross-layer variation has systematic structure.
key_risks: Absorption graph is inconsistent across SAE runs (Song et al. 2025 shows 0.80 feature consistency); cross-layer profile shows no systematic structure
candidates.pivot_trigger: Front-runner theory AUROC < 0.65 AND cross-domain result is null

candidates.title: Systematic Mitigation Benchmark
candidates.summary: Controlled head-to-head comparison of absorption mitigation methods (Matryoshka, OrtSAE, ATM SAE, masked regularization) on the same model, layer, and metric suite. First paper to compare all major methods fairly, revealing trade-offs that are currently invisible due to inconsistent evaluation protocols.
hypotheses: No single mitigation method dominates across all absorption metrics AND downstream tasks (sparse probing, RAVEL disentanglement, reconstruction loss)
Absorption reduction from Matryoshka does not fully generalize to entity-type hierarchies (reduction is architecture-specific)
OrtSAE provides the best absorption-reconstruction balance without the reconstruction penalty of Matryoshka
candidates.pilot_focus: 15-minute pilot: load Matryoshka vs. TopK Gemma Scope SAEs (both pre-trained and available via SAEBench), compare absorption rate on first-letter task, reconstruction CE delta.
key_risks: Purely empirical without conceptual insight -- low ceiling for top-venue acceptance without theoretical framing from cand_a

candidates.title: Deconfounding Measurement + Seed Dependence + Ensemble Methods
candidates.summary: Contrarian-inspired backup testing whether measured absorption rates are inflated by confounds: (1) L0 misspecification (feature hedging masquerading as absorption), (2) seed-dependent decomposition artifacts, (3) evaluation on artificial hierarchies. Proposes rigorous 'Absorption Stress Test' protocol and tests ensemble methods as a training-free mitigation.
hypotheses: At least 30% of measured absorption is attributable to L0 misspecification rather than true sparsity-driven absorption
Seed-invariant absorption rate (absorbed in >=80% of seeds) is less than 50% of single-seed absorption rate
An ensemble of K>=3 SAEs (same architecture, different seeds) achieves lower absorption than any individual SAE
candidates.pilot_focus: 20-minute pilot: run 'Sparse but Wrong' L0 diagnostic on one Gemma Scope SAE. Measure absorption at standard vs. corrected L0. Check whether corrected L0 substantially reduces measured absorption rate.
candidates.target_venue: NeurIPS 2026 measurement standards track or main track
key_risks: If H1 and H2 are both falsified (absorption is robust and seed-consistent), the paper becomes a replication study -- still publishable as rigorous validation
Multi-seed training required for full seed-dependence analysis
candidates.pivot_trigger: Contrarian concerns deserve standalone paper, or as methodological controls paper regardless of front-runner outcome

perspective_weights.theoretical: HIGH -- provided rigorous proof structure, biconvex decomposition argument, exact impossibility theorem. Core mathematical structure adopted.
perspective_weights.innovator: HIGH -- rate-distortion framing, ASI concept, cross-domain hypothesis. Highest novelty estimates for theoretical contributions.
perspective_weights.empiricist: HIGH -- provided rigorous evaluation design (shuffled controls, Bonferroni correction, probe quality gates, bootstrap CIs, random-SAE baseline). All controls adopted.
perspective_weights.pragmatist: MEDIUM-HIGH -- feasibility validation, sae-spelling + RAVEL code path, L0 diagnostic control from 'Sparse but Wrong' paper. Critical engineering contribution.
perspective_weights.contrarian: MEDIUM -- L0 deconfounding control incorporated as Phase C.4; seed-dependence concern preserved in backup cand_d; 'absorption heterogeneity' insight retained as interpretive nuance.
perspective_weights.interdisciplinary: MEDIUM -- phase transition framework and immunodominance analogy contributed. Phase transition dynamics incorporated as Phase E. Hysteresis test retained. Immunodominance analogy retained as interpretive framing in discussion section (not as primary contribution).

key_conflicts_resolved: Innovator proposed training-free analysis only; Theoretical and Interdisciplinary proposed hysteresis test requiring fine-tuning. Resolution: hysteresis test allowed as one 60-minute experiment on GPT-2 Small (small training cost, high scientific value for confirming/rejecting metastability).

Pragmatist proposed cross-domain as primary contribution; Innovator proposed rate-distortion theory as primary. Resolution: they are complementary -- theory motivates empirical design, cross-domain validates and extends theory. Both are primary.

Contrarian challenged whether measured absorption rates are reliable. Resolution: L0 deconfounding control (Phase C.4 using 'Sparse but Wrong' metric) directly addresses this. The contrarian's deeper paradigm challenge is acknowledged but scoped to discussion section.

Interdisciplinary proposed immunodominance score (affinity ratio × residual variance ratio) as a novel predictor. Resolution: the immunodominance score reduces to ASI = cos^2(theta) × (freq_p / freq_c) under simplification; retained as interpretive framing connecting to immunology literature.

Empiricist demanded rigorous null controls (shuffled labels, random-SAE baseline, Bonferroni correction, bootstrap CIs). Resolution: all controls fully incorporated as non-negotiable methodological standards.

Theoretical perspective proposed Information-Theoretic Lower Bound as a secondary contribution. Resolution: dropped due to proof soundness issues (the lambda × H(z_p | z_c) subtraction is not rigorous); retained as intuitive motivation for the rate-distortion framing.
dropped_ideas: Candidate B (Ecological Competitive Exclusion / intransitivity index) -- structural correspondence too loose, computational cost prohibitive, formal predictions lack backing. The competitive exclusion intuition is retained as interpretive framing within the Lotka-Volterra coexistence condition in the appendix.
Information-Theoretic Lower Bound as primary contribution -- proof soundness issues (lambda × H(z_p | z_c) term not rigorously derived from information theory); retained as intuitive motivation.
Feature Anchoring as primary contribution (cand_d from prior round) -- Tang et al. (2512.05534) has already validated anchoring broadly; narrow residual novelty only.
Reconstruction-only mitigation benchmark as primary contribution -- demoted to backup status (cand_c).
Lateral Inhibition SAE as primary experimental contribution -- requires training, violates training-free preference; retained as a theoretical discussion point about principled fixes.
key_novel_predictions: Co-occurrence frequency cancels from the absorption threshold -- absorption risk depends only on decoder geometry and sparsity, not on how often features co-occur. This is the most surprising and falsifiable single prediction.
Absorbed state may be metastable (hysteresis) -- post-hoc sparsity reduction cannot reliably reverse absorption. This has direct practical implications for mitigation strategy.
Cross-domain absorption should exist in entity-type and geographic hierarchies -- if confirmed, dramatically expands the known scope of absorption; if null, refines it.
The impossibility theorem: h* = O(1/sqrt(lambda)) -- any hierarchy deeper than this critical depth will exhibit absorption regardless of SAE width or training duration.
